steps.title: Map the first half of the text
steps.content: The opening sentences present Lucy Sprague Mitchell's view that books for very young children should imitate how toddlers use language — a philosophy about what is most suitable for that audience.

steps.title: Map the second half of the text
steps.content: The remainder of the passage offers Goodnight Moon as the "most enduring example" of that idea and notes that its repetition, rhythm, and nonsense rhyme "speak directly to Mitchell's influence." So a specific book is then described as embodying the earlier philosophy.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'outlines a debate between two authors... and then traces how that debate shaped theories': there is no debate between Mitchell and Brown; Brown's book illustrates Mitchell's idea. ✗
  • Choice B — 'summarizes an argument... and then discusses a contrasting view': the second half does not contrast with the first; it exemplifies it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'lists the literary characteristics... common to many classics': only one book is discussed, not many classics, and no general list of characteristics is given. ✗
  • Choice D — 'presents a philosophy... and then describes a book influenced by that philosophy': exactly matches Mitchell's idea followed by Goodnight Moon. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
